NUL.def: Removal, negation, voiding — making something absent

prototypes.reason: Input: state with something present. Output: state with that thing absent. A gap is created where something was.
Input: existing thing. Output: that thing absent. Makes something void.
Input: state with unwanted thing present; Output: state with that thing absent; makes absence explicit by removing something.
Input: operating process. Output: process absent, stopped. Gap where operation was.
Input: thing present; Output: thing eliminated/absent; makes removal explicit.

sample_output_patterns: same state with contact/success made explicit as absent, gap marked
same state with thirst/pain made absent
same context with process now absent (ceased)
experiencer with those expectations now unmet/absent
person with hostility now absent (won over)

DES.def: Naming, typing, marking — assigning identity or category

prototypes.reason: Input: unnamed thing. Output: thing with identifying term. Category or label assigned.
Input: thing without explicit definition; Output: thing with definitional boundary drawn; assigns essential nature
Input: unnamed thing, Output: thing with assigned role/identity, Change: category/label assigned
Input: thing without sanctioned status. Output: thing designated as acceptable. Approval label assigned.
Input: thing untyped; Output: thing designated as having certain characteristic; assigns definitional property.

sample_output_patterns: entity now designated as god or godlike, categorical identity assigned
statement now designated as true by speaker, categorical epistemic label assigned
statement re-designated as true, categorical label reasserted

INS.def: Creating, appearing, bringing into being — new existence

prototypes.reason: Input: existing state. Output: new state appears. The verb instantiates a transformed condition.
Input: information/decision exists. Output: new action/behavior appears in response. New phenomenon entering the world.
Input: person outside; Output: person + entering movement; the entry action is a new phenomenon appearing.
Input: person in background. Output: person visible/prominent with action taken. Forward movement and visibility arise as new observable state.
Input: position A. Output: position closer to target. A movement trajectory appears.

sample_output_patterns: two entities co-present in space as a transient event, no persistent structural link created
accompanying movement event occurs, a new phenomenon (the escorting action) enters the world without building persistent structure
guiding action as new event appears, a transient helping phenomenon rather than structural transformation
tending behavior appears as ongoing activity event, not a structural link being built
directing action appears as new control event, embodied action arising
admiration feeling arises in experiencer, new psychological state appearing
intense love feeling arises in experiencer, new emotional state instantiated
value increase appears as new property state, thing gains additional worth

prototypes.reason: Input: whole. Output: that whole divided into parts or portions. Partitioning transformation.
Input: whole. Output: multiple parts. Boundaries drawn through to create divisions.
Input: whole. Output: parts separated by boundaries. Thing partitioned into segments.
Input: integrated whole. Output: separated parts. Partitions a mass by drawing boundaries and creating distinct subsets.
Input: whole object; output: parts separated by breaking, boundary drawn through whole.

sample_output_patterns: that state divided into disordered parts
that state fragmented into confused parts
parts separated by boundary drawn through it
container breached, contents dispersed into parts

CON.def: Linking, relating, joining — building persistent connections

prototypes.reason: Input: two unattached things. Output: things attached with persistent link. Structural connection built.
Input: two unconnected logical/causal entities. Output: those entities with a persistent conceptual link. A structural connection is built.
Input: separate entities. Output: entities with persistent interconnection links. Multiple structural connections built.
Input: two unlinked entities. Output: entities with persistent structural link. A connection is built.
Input: thing and whole unlinked; output: persistent structural part-whole relationship established.

sample_output_patterns: individuals with persistent team membership links

SYN.def: Combining, merging, unifying — creating wholes from parts

prototypes.reason: Input: separate components; Output: unified assembly transcending parts; components merged into new whole.
Input: separate elements. Output: unified synthesis exists. Components merged into transcendent whole.
Input: multiple components; output: unified product transcending parts; combines elements into emergent whole.
Input: separate elements. Output: unified whole transcending parts. Elements synthesized into new integrated entity.
Input: separate things. Output: unified whole. Multiple elements synthesized into one.

ALT.def: Switching perspective, reframing — same thing, different lens

prototypes.reason: Input: thing with one explanatory frame. Output: same thing with different explanatory frame. Lens changes.
Input: thing + proportional frame. Output: same thing + excessive frame. Magnitude reinterpreted beyond truth.
Input: text in original frame. Output: same text with new understanding frame. Lens shifts on existing content.
Input: activity in one mode. Output: same activity in different mode. The frame shifts between perspectives.
Input: system in arrangement A. Output: system in arrangement B. Shifts operational frame.

sample_input_patterns: entities in positions a and b
thing a seen as a, thing b seen as b
sample_output_patterns: same entities in positions b and a
same person now holding frame y (truth realization)
same service viewed/accessed at tier x+1,

prototypes.reason: Input: resolved position. Output: state oscillating between incompatible positions. The verb maintains contradictory values coexisting.
Input: resolved state. Output: incompatible values coexist. The verb creates condition where opposing things are simultaneously present.
Input: resolved state. Output: state where incompatible choices coexist. Holds contradiction between options.
Input: resolved claim. Output: incompatible claims held together. Creates state where opposing values coexist.
Input: resolved state. Output: state where contradictory positions coexist; actively maintaining incompatibility between two things.

sample_output_patterns: unresolved state holding incompatible positions simultaneously,
state holding multiple incompatible values simultaneously
state holding multiple incompatible positions simultaneously

REC.def: Self-referential restructuring — rebuilding from held tension

prototypes.reason: Input: organization with structure A; Output: organization with structure B; the structural relationships are rebuilt.
Input: existing organizational structure; Output: structure rebuilt around new organizing principles.
Input: existing arrangement. Output: reorganized arrangement. The spatial structure rebuilds around new positions.
Input: thing with one structure. Output: thing with fundamentally different structure. The architecture reorganizes around new center.
Input: outdated structure. Output: restructured modern form. The thing is rebuilt around contemporary standards.

sample_output_patterns: structure reorganized with new adaptive features
same elements reorganized around aesthetic principle
same content reorganized around new structure
structure reorganized around new focal point
